extraction_reasoning: The litigation table separates matters by direction and category: I counted only the “Against” rows—criminal proceedings against the Company (283), Subsidiaries (15), Directors (8), Key Managerial Personnel (8) and Senior Management (2), totaling 316; statutory/regulatory proceedings against the Company (6) and Subsidiaries (3), totaling 9; and tax proceedings against the Company (105) and Subsidiaries (14), totaling 119. I excluded every “By” row and excluded the Promoter’s personal tax proceedings from the company/group tax count; the quantified aggregate against the Company and Subsidiaries is ₹7,645.6 million plus ₹477.1 million = ₹8,122.7 million, or ₹812.27 crore. The contingent-liabilities figure uses the explicitly stated consolidated “Total” of ₹7,889.2 million as at June 30, 2025, rather than summing line items, equal to ₹788.92 crore. No company-wide top-five or top-ten customer revenue concentration percentage is disclosed; the only relevant percentages are business/loan portfolio metrics, not customer concentration. The litigation and contingent-liability tables use ₹ million, converted to crore by dividing by 10.
